steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage lists two separate actions showing Idár fighting injustice: (1) voicing support for the Mexican revolt as a reporter, and (2) founding the League of Mexican Women. The blank connects them — the second action must be presented as an addition to the first, not a conclusion, example, or contrast.

steps.title: Test each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Additionally,’: signals that founding the League is another separate act of fighting injustice — exactly the additive relationship the passage establishes. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘In conclusion,’: signals a summary or wrap-up — founding the League is a new action, not a conclusion of the reporting work. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘For example,’: signals that founding the League illustrates the previous point about voicing support for the revolt — but these are two distinct acts, not one exemplifying the other. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Rather,’: signals contrast or correction — nothing in the passage contradicts what came before; both actions reinforce the same theme. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
